title: नटराज स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ अथ श्रीनटराजस्तोत्रम् ॥

सदञ्चितमुदञ्चितनिकुञ्चितपदं झलझलञ्चलितमञ्जुकटकम्।
पतञ्जलिदृगञ्जनमनञ्जनमचञ्चलपदं जनन भञ्जनकरम्।
कदम्बरुचिमम्बरवसं परममम्बुदकदम्बककबन्धकबरम्।
चिदम्बरनटं हृदि भजे स्फुरदरम्भम-नतारम-शिवम्बरनटम्॥१॥

हरं त्रिपुरभञ्जनमनन्तकृतकंकणविराजितकरम्।
परं परमशम्भुमुमयार्धवपुषं नमत सम्भ्रमनिबद्धगरलम्।
कपालमालाधरमशेषसुरसुन्दरमशेषगणनायकयुतम्।
चिदम्बरनटं हृदि भजे हृदयमम्बरगतं सदयमम्बुजदलम्॥२॥

क्वणन्मणिमयूखशिखिमौलिविलसन्मणिकिरीटधरगंगम्।
विलोलविलसद्भुजगकुण्डलविलासितसुकर्णविभवम्।
कनत्कनककुण्डलधरं कनकशैलनिभगौरवपुषम्।
चिदम्बरनटं हृदि भजे मदनकोटिनिभचारुवदनम्॥३॥

ज्वलत्कनकमण्डलमुखे लसदनलललाटहुतभुक्प्रकटम्।
वलन्मृदुमृणालकमलोत्तमलसत्पदनखेन्दुविभवम्।
रणन्मणिघटामणिनिकट्टकतटीयुगशिवांगवसनम्।
चिदम्बरनटं हृदि भजे निजरसानुभवसन्मयसभम्॥४॥

पिनाकिनमनाकमुकमादिपुरुषं सकलविश्वमथनम्।
विनायकगुहान्वितमुमाधिपमथो जगतामीश्वरनुतम्।
वृषारूढमशेषगणसेवितमनेकविधनृत्तकलयम्।
चिदम्बरनटं हृदि भजे जगदुदीरणकरं सततनटम्॥५॥

उदग्रललाटनयनार्चिषमिव ज्वलदनंगशलभम्।
वनान्तवनवासमथ दिक्पटधरं त्रिदशवन्दितपदम्।
विषादहरमात्मविदां वितरणं सकलसम्पदामीश्वरम्।
चिदम्बरनटं हृदि भजे ह्यनवरतनटनं परशिवम्॥६॥

॥ इति श्रीनटराजस्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Atha Shri Nataraja Stotram ||

Sadanchitam-Udanchita-Nikunchita-Padam Jhala-Jhalanchalita-Manju-Katakam.
Patanjali-Driganjanam-Ananjanam-Achanchala-Padam Janana-Bhanjanakaram.
Kadambaruchim-Ambaravasam Paramam-Ambudakadambaka-Kabandha-Kabaram.
Chidambara-Natam Hridi Bhaje Sphuradarambham-Anataaram-Shivambara-Natam.||1||

Haram Tripurabhanjanam-Anantakrita-Kankana-Virajita-Karam.
Param Paramashambhum-Umayaardhavapusham Namata Sambhrama-Nibaddha-Garalam.
Kapaala-Maala-Dharam-Ashesha-Sura-Sundaram-Ashesha-Gana-Nayaka-Yutam.
Chidambara-Natam Hridi Bhaje Hridayam-Ambaragatam Sadayam-Ambujadalam.||2||

Kvanan-Mani-Mayookha-Shikhi-Mauli-Vilasan-Mani-Kireeta-Dhara-Gangam.
Vilolavilasad-Bhujaga-Kundala-Vilaasita-Sukarna-Vibhavam.
Kanat-Kanaka-Kundala-Dharam Kanaka-Shaila-Nibha-Gaura-Vapusham.
Chidambara-Natam Hridi Bhaje Madanakoti-Nibha-Chaaru-Vadanam.||3||

Jvalat-Kanaka-Mandala-Mukhe Lasad-Anala-Lalaata-Hutabhuk-Prakatam.
Valan-Mridu-Mrinaala-Kamala-Uttama-Lasat-Pada-Nakhendu-Vibhavam.
Ranan-Mani-Ghataa-Mani-Nikattaka-Tatee-Yuga-Shivaanga-Vasanam.
Chidambara-Natam Hridi Bhaje Nijarasaanubhava-Sanmaya-Sabham.||4||

Pinaakinam-Anaakam-Ukam-Aadi-Purusham Sakala-Vishva-Mathanam.
Vinaayaka-Guhaanvitam-Umaadhipam-Atho Jagataam-Eeshvara-Nutam.
Vrishaaaroodham-Ashesha-Gana-Sevitam-Aneka-Vidha-Nritta-Kalayam.
Chidambara-Natam Hridi Bhaje Jagad-Udeerana-Karam Satata-Natam.||5||

Udagra-Lalaata-Nayanarchisham-Iva Jvalad-Ananga-Shalabham.
Vanaanta-Vana-Vaasam-Atha Dikpata-Dharam Tridasha-Vandita-Padam.
Vishaadaharam-Aatmavidaam Vitaranam Sakala-Sampadaam-Eeshvaram.
Chidambara-Natam Hridi Bhaje Hy-Anavarata-Natanam Parashivam.||6||

|| Iti Shri Nataraja Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
१. सदा उठा हुआ, ऊपर उठा हुआ, मुड़ा हुआ चरण, झलझल बजते मञ्जु कटक (कड़े), पतञ्जलि की आँखों का अंजन, निर्लिप्त, अचंचल चरण, जन्म-मृत्यु का भञ्जन करने वाले, कदम्ब जैसी कान्ति, अम्बर (आकाश) वस्त्र, मेघ जैसी जटा — ऐसे चिदम्बर के नटराज को हृदय में भजता हूँ, शिव-आकाश में नृत्य करने वाले।

२. हर (शिव), त्रिपुरभञ्जन, अनन्तनाग के कंकण से विराजित करों वाले, परम शम्भु, उमा के अर्धवपु, गरल कण्ठ में बाँधे हुए, कपालमाला धारी, सभी देवों में सुन्दर, सभी गणनायकों से युक्त — ऐसे चिदम्बर नट को भजता हूँ।

३. बजती मणियों की किरणों से शिखा-मौलि पर विलसती मणिकिरीट और गंगा, लहराते सर्पकुण्डल से शोभित कान, चमकते स्वर्ण-कुण्डल, कनकगिरि जैसे गौर शरीर, करोड़ों कामदेव जैसे सुन्दर मुख वाले चिदम्बर नट को भजता हूँ।

४. जलते स्वर्ण-मण्डल में ललाट-अग्नि प्रकट, कोमल मृणाल-कमल जैसे उत्तम चरण-नखचन्द्र, बजती मणिमालाएँ — ऐसे आत्मरसानुभव से पूर्ण सभा में नटराज को भजता हूँ।

५. पिनाकधारी, आदिपुरुष, सकल विश्व का मथन करने वाले, विनायक-कार्तिकेय सहित, उमाधिपति, जगत के ईश्वर, वृषभारूढ, समस्त गणसेवित, अनेक प्रकार की नृत्यकला — जगत को उत्पन्न करने वाले सतत नट चिदम्बर नट को भजता हूँ।

६. ऊर्ध्व ललाट नेत्र की अर्चि से कामदेव को पतंगे की भाँति भस्म करने वाले, वनवासी, दिगम्बर, त्रिदेवों द्वारा वन्दित, आत्मविदों के विषाद हरने वाले, सकल सम्पदाओं के ईश्वर — अनवरत नर्तन करने वाले परशिव, चिदम्बर नट को भजता हूँ।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीनटराजस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य परम्परागत ऋषिः। दण्डक / शार्दूलविक्रीडित छन्दः। श्री नटराज (शिव) देवता। ॐ नमः शिवाय बीजम्। नृत्य शक्तिः। चिदम्बर नटराज प्रसाद कीलकम्। श्रीनटराजप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Invokes Lord Shiva in His cosmic dancer (Nataraja) form
Invokes artistic talent and mastery in dance and music
Traditionally recited for removal of the cycle of birth and death (janana-bhanjana)
Invokes the blessing of the vision of cosmic dance — creation and dissolution
Particularly sacred for devotees of Chidambaram temple
Traditionally recited for removal of grief, sorrow, and bestows all forms of prosperity
Invokes spiritual knowledge through the metaphor of divine dance
description: Nataraja Stotram (नटराज स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Lord Shiva. This sacred composition is sourced from Traditional Sanskrit Hymn (Chidambaram tradition) and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to bestow the vision of cosmic dance — creation and dissolution, remove grief, sorrow, and bestow all forms of prosperity, and grant artistic talent and mastery in dance and music. This sacred text is ideally recited on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri, Arudra Darshanam, and Shravan month for enhanced blessings. Comprising 6 verses, it can be completed in approximately 6 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Nataraja Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Nataraja Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Praise of Lord Shiva as the cosmic dancer Nataraja for spiritual wisdom and divine grace.

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Nataraja Stotram and when?
seoFaqs.answer: The most celebrated Nataraja Stotram is attributed to Sage Patanjali, the same rishi credited with the Yoga Sutras and the Mahabhashya commentary on Panini's grammar. According to the Chidambaram sthala-purana, Patanjali travelled to Tillai (Chidambaram) to behold Shiva's Ananda Tandava and composed this hymn spontaneously when granted darshan. The verses are preserved in Shaiva agamic literature and have been chanted for over a thousand years in the Chidambaram Nataraja temple. (Source: Chidambaram Mahatmya; Shaivam.org; Vaidika Vignanam.)

seoFaqs.question: What is unique about the language of this stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Nataraja Stotram is famous for a linguistic constraint Patanjali imposed on himself: the words charana (foot, leg) and shringa (horn) do not appear anywhere in the hymn. Tradition holds that Patanjali, born with serpent's coils for legs, avoided charana out of self-effacement and avoided shringa to playfully tease Nandi, the horned bull who initially blocked his path to the sanctum. This grammatical austerity, combined with rich poetic metre, makes the stotra a tour-de-force of Sanskrit composition. (Source: Raja Thatha's blog on Chidambaram; Stotra Nidhi commentary.)

seoFaqs.question: What does Nataraja symbolise in this hymn?
seoFaqs.answer: Each verse meditates on Shiva as Nataraja, the cosmic dancer whose Ananda Tandava performs the five divine acts: srishti (creation), sthiti (preservation), samhara (dissolution), tirobhava (concealment), and anugraha (grace). The flames around the dancing form represent the cosmos, the drum (damaru) symbolises primordial sound, the raised foot offers liberation, and the dwarf trampled underneath is Apasmara, the demon of ignorance. The stotra invites the devotee to perceive the entire universe arising and dissolving within Shiva's rhythm. (Source: Coomaraswamy 'The Dance of Shiva'; Chidambaram temple agama.)

seoFaqs.question: How many verses are in the Nataraja Stotram and how should it be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Most published recensions contain eight to nine Sanskrit verses, each ending with the refrain 'natarajaraja namo namaste' or a similar salutation. It is traditionally recited at dawn before a Nataraja icon or image of the Chidambaram sanctum, often accompanied by simple bell and lamp offerings. Devotees in Shaiva Siddhanta lineages chant it at the conclusion of pradosha worship and during Arudra Darshanam in the Tamil month of Margazhi, when Nataraja's anointment ceremony takes place. (Source: Shaivam.org; Templesinindiainfo.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits do devotees seek from chanting this stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional commentaries state that recitation of the Patanjali Nataraja Stotram bestows liberation from the cycle of birth and death, victory over ignorance (the trampled Apasmara), and the experience of inner stillness amid outer activity. Yoga practitioners chant it to align breath with the cosmic rhythm Shiva embodies, while seekers of healing recite it because Patanjali, also revered as the patron of Ayurveda and yoga, composed it. The hymn is widely held to be especially efficacious when chanted at the Chidambaram sanctum itself. (Source: Bhaktinidhi; Shlokam.org.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is the Chidambaram temple so central to this stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Chidambaram is regarded as the akasha-linga sthala, where Shiva is worshipped as the formless ether (chidambara-rahasya) inside the Chit Sabha, the Hall of Consciousness. The bronze Nataraja icon at Chidambaram is considered the prototype of all Nataraja images worldwide. The Patanjali Stotram is sung daily as part of the temple's six-time puja schedule, and the relationship between Patanjali, Vyaghrapada, and Nataraja is depicted in temple sculpture, making the stotra inseparable from the Chidambaram tradition. (Source: Chidambaram Mahatmya; temple priest interviews compiled in Shaivam.org.)
